description:
Goal
Introduce compiler::World as the authoritative mutable compiler state and move execution-graph preparation onto it so Compiler stages populate world state instead of returning PreparedExecutionGraph.

Signal
- compiler::World owns source-map, diagnostics, linked units, module, and module-plan state needed after execution-graph preparation.
- Compiler frontend and execution-graph entrypoints mutate a supplied World.
- PreparedExecutionGraph is removed or reduced to an implementation detail no longer visible at the compiler boundary.
- Compiler and pipeline tests cover the new world state after preparation.

Strategy
- Define World and its accessors in src/compiler.rs.
- Rework modules::pipeline preparation to fill world-owned execution state.
- Update compiler tests first, then production callers that only need execution-graph data.
title: Compiler World: define authoritative state and world-owned execution graph
